name: sheets_batch_update
description: Apply a raw Google Sheets API batchUpdate to a spreadsheet for full structural editing (add/delete/rename sheets, formatting, merges, conditional formatting, freezing rows, writing cells, etc). You supply the requests array exactly as the Google Sheets API expects it; it is passed through verbatim. Use read_sheet (no range) first to get sheet titles and sheet_id values to target.

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID to edit.

requests.description: a Google Sheets API batchUpdate `requests` array (full structural editing). Each item is one Sheets request object (e.g. addSheet, repeatCell, updateCells, mergeCells, deleteSheet).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

returns: JSON with spreadsheet_id, applied (request count), and the API replies array (or an error string).
notes:
requests is the RAW Google Sheets API array, passed through verbatim. Worked examples:

1) Add a new sheet/tab:
[{"addSheet": {"properties": {"title": "Summary"}}}]

2) Bold the header row of sheet 0 (repeatCell over a range):
[{"repeatCell": {"range": {"sheetId": 0, "startRowIndex": 0, "endRowIndex": 1}, "cell": {"userEnteredFormat": {"textFormat": {"bold": true}}}, "fields": "userEnteredFormat.textFormat.bold"}}]

3) Write values into cells (updateCells, 0-based indices):
[{"updateCells": {"start": {"sheetId": 0, "rowIndex": 0, "columnIndex": 0}, "rows": [{"values": [{"userEnteredValue": {"stringValue": "Name"}}, {"userEnteredValue": {"stringValue": "Total"}}]}], "fields": "userEnteredValue"}}]

The sheetId comes from read_sheet (sheet_id). For simply writing a block of values, update_sheet_values (A1 + USER_ENTERED) is simpler than updateCells.
